text:
In a small, sleepy town named Afternoonton, there lived a quirky old man named Harold Crimsongear. He was known not just for his eccentric habit of wearing socks indoors but for his extraordinary memory. Harold could recollect events that happened decades ago with uncanny precision.

One sunny afternoon, Harold decided to clean out his long-forgotten attic. Among the dusty trinkets and forgotten toys, he found a box labeled "Mystery Papers." He opened it, and from within poked a score of yellowed papers.

His eyes narrowed behind his bifocals. "The Great Egg Roll Controversy," he murmured to himself, reading the headlines. Apparently, there was a giant egg at the local fair, and it rolled so furiously that it knocked over several prized items. The town gathered, hoping for a re-enactment or even the next egg. Someone had brought a truckload of fake feathers to distract it, but the egg won anyway.

D
